title: excoecaria bantamensis
intro: Excoecaria bantamensis, also known as the poison tree, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, characterized by its toxic sap and yellowish-green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Excoecaria bantamensis include its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its ability to reduce fever and relieve pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a paste or ointment, applied topically to affected areas to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
